perturbation.original_text: Personal Technical Support 24 x 7 emergency phone support and 1-business-day email response on non-critical issues. Includes a 99 percent server uptime guarantee.
perturbation.changed_text: Personal Technical Support 24 x 7 emergency phone support and 3-business-day email response on non-critical issues. Includes a 95 percent server uptime guarantee.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the guaranteed server uptime from 99% to 95% and the email response time from 1-business day to 3-business days. This creates uncertainty because the actual service level is now ambiguous, potentially conflicting with expectations set elsewhere in the agreement regarding performance and support response times.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Personal Technical Support 24 x 7 emergency phone support and 3-business-day email response on non-critical issues. Includes a 95 percent server uptime guarantee.
perturbation.justification: The original text guaranteed 99% server uptime and a 1-business-day email response. The changed text reduces the uptime guarantee to 95% and extends the email response to 3-business-days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies 95% uptime and a 3-business-day email response. Since these service levels are directly inconsistent, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' is met, as different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The effective term is 12 months beginning March 1, 2005 and ending February 28, 2006.
perturbation.changed_text: The effective term is 12 months beginning March 1, 2005 and ending February 28, 2007.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the agreement's end date from February 28, 2006 to February 28, 2007 directly contradicts the original term length (12 months). Now the end date extends beyond the originally specified period, leading to uncertainty about when the agreement actually terminates and affecting clauses related to renewal, termination fees, or obligations tied to the original term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The effective term is 12 months beginning March 1, 2005 and ending February 28, 2007.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement's end date from February 28, 2006 to February 28, 2007. According to the explanation, this change contradicts the originally specified term length (12 months). Because the end date now extends beyond the originally specified period, it leads to uncertainty about when the agreement actually terminates. This affects clauses related to renewal, termination fees, or obligations tied to the original term and is also directly contradictory as the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but it now conflicts with the original intention of a 12 month contract. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
